categories.name: أَسْمَاءُ الْأَنْبِيَاءِ فِي سُورَةِ الصَّافَّاتِ
categories.nameEn: Names of Prophets in Surah As-Saffat

items.verseEn: And Noah had certainly called Us, and [We are] the best of responders.
And indeed, among his kind was Abraham,
And We gave him good tidings of Isaac, a prophet from among the righteous.
And We did certainly confer favor upon Moses and Aaron.

categories.name: أَرْبَعَةُ أَشْيَاءٍ مُبَارَكَةٍ فِي الْقُرْآنِ
categories.nameEn: Things Described as Blessed in the Quran

items.verse: إِنَّآ أَنزَلْنَـٰهُ فِى لَيْلَةٍۢ مُّبَـٰرَكَةٍ ۚ إِنَّا كُنَّا مُنذِرِينَ
items.verseEn: Indeed, We sent it down during a blessed night. Indeed, We were to warn [mankind].

items.verse: وَهَـٰذَا كِتَـٰبٌ أَنزَلْنَـٰهُ مُبَارَكٌۭ مُّصَدِّقُ ٱلَّذِى بَيْنَ يَدَيْهِ وَلِتُنذِرَ أُمَّ ٱلْقُرَىٰ وَمَنْ حَوْلَهَا ۚ وَٱلَّذِينَ يُؤْمِنُونَ بِٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ يُؤْمِنُونَ بِهِۦ ۖ وَهُمْ عَلَىٰ صَلَاتِهِمْ يُحَافِظُونَ
items.verseEn: And this is a Book which We have sent down, blessed and confirming what was before it, that you may warn the Mother of Cities [i.e., Makkah] and those around it. Those who believe in the Hereafter believe in it, and they are maintaining their prayers.

items.verse: فَلَمَّآ أَتَىٰهَا نُودِىَ مِن شَـٰطِئِ ٱلْوَادِ ٱلْأَيْمَنِ فِى ٱلْبُقْعَةِ ٱلْمُبَـٰرَكَةِ مِنَ ٱلشَّجَرَةِ أَن يَـٰمُوسَىٰٓ إِنِّىٓ أَنَا ٱللَّهُ رَبُّ ٱلْعَـٰلَمِينَ
items.verseEn: But when he came to it, he was called from the right side of the valley in a blessed spot - from the tree, "O Moses, indeed I am Allāh, Lord of the worlds."

items.verse: وَقُل رَّبِّ أَنزِلْنِى مُنزَلًۭا مُّبَارَكًۭا وَأَنتَ خَيْرُ ٱلْمُنزِلِينَ
items.verseEn: And say, 'My Lord, let me land at a blessed landing place, and You are the best to accommodate [us].'"

categories.name: صِفَاتُ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ فِي سُورَةِ الْمُؤْمِنُونَ
categories.nameEn: Qualities of the Believers in Surah Al-Mu'minun

items.verseEn: And they who are observant of zakāh
And they who guard their private parts
And they who are to their trusts and their promises attentive
And they who carefully maintain their prayers -

items.verse: ٱلطَّلَـٰقُ مَرَّتَانِ ۖ فَإِمْسَاكٌۢ بِمَعْرُوفٍ أَوْ تَسْرِيحٌۢ بِإِحْسَـٰنٍۢ ۗ وَلَا يَحِلُّ لَكُمْ أَن تَأْخُذُوا۟ مِمَّآ ءَاتَيْتُمُوهُنَّ شَيْـًٔا إِلَّآ أَن يَخَافَآ أَلَّا يُقِيمَا حُدُودَ ٱللَّهِ ۖ فَإِنْ خِفْتُمْ أَلَّا يُقِيمَا حُدُودَ ٱللَّهِ فَلَا جُنَاحَ عَلَيْهِمَا فِيمَا ٱفْتَدَتْ بِهِۦ ۗ تِلْكَ حُدُودُ ٱللَّهِ فَلَا تَعْتَدُوهَا ۚ وَمَن يَتَعَدَّ حُدُودَ ٱللَّهِ فَأُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ هُمُ ٱلظَّـٰلِمُونَ
items.verseEn: Divorce is twice. Then [after that], either keep [her] in an acceptable manner or release [her] with good treatment. And it is not lawful for you to take anything of what you have given them unless both fear that they will not be able to keep [within] the limits of Allāh. But if you fear that they will not keep [within] the limits of Allāh, then there is no blame upon either of them concerning that by which she ransoms herself. These are the limits of Allāh, so do not transgress them. And whoever transgresses the limits of Allāh - it is those who are the wrongdoers [i.e., the unjust].
And below them both [in excellence] are two [other] gardens -
In both of them are two springs, spouting.
[He is] Lord of the two sunrises and Lord of the two sunsets.

harf.hint: The sun is described as this blazing source of light in the Quran

deduction.title: The Voice That Challenged an Idol's Reign
deduction.intro: Among the messengers of the Almighty was a steadfast servant who rose among a stubborn nation to deliver the truth. Their people had adopted a fabricated deity, turning away from the Creator of all that exists. With unwavering resolve, this individual called them back — but the message was met with denial and rejection. Step through the clues to discover who this devoted servant was.
clues: I was sent as a messenger to a stubborn people who had wandered far from the straight path.
I came to remind my nation of the One who created all that exists — the best of all creators.
My people had turned to a false idol they venerated and called upon day and night.
I boldly challenged them, asking why they would worship a fabricated deity and abandon their true Lord.
They denied me, refused to listen, and held fast to their falsehood — yet my Lord preserved me.
I am Ilyas — and peace was granted upon me, and I was counted among the believing servants of the Most Merciful.

identity.label: Who Am I?
options: Prophet Ilyas
Prophet Idris
Prophet Alyasa
Prophet Dhul-Kifl
Prophet Nuh

trial.label: Trial/Event
options: Calling a people away from the worship of Ba'al
Confronting a tyrant who claimed to be a god
Warning a people about an impending flood
Being thrown into a great blazing fire
Leading an exodus from the land of bondage
trial.answer: Calling a people away from the worship of Ba'al

location.label: Key Element
options: The idol named Ba'al
A great ark built from sturdy wood
A staff that parted a vast sea
A sacred garment stained with blood
A burning bush on a holy mount

outcome.label: Outcome
options: Granted peace and counted among the chosen servants of Allah
Led into paradise while all others were destroyed
Rescued from a great fire that became cool and safe
Given dominion over jinn, humanity, and the wind
Body preserved as a lasting sign for the ages
outcome.answer: Granted peace and counted among the chosen servants of Allah

translations: From time that We ordained on (the) Children (of) Israel that he
who kills a soul other than (for) a soul or (for) spreading corruption in
the earth then (it) is as if he has killed mankind all and whoever saves it then (it) is as if
he has saved mankind all And surely came to them Our Messengers with clear Signs yet
indeed many of them after that in the earth (are) surely those who commit excesses
scramble.hint: The sanctity of human life — the gravity of taking one innocent life is as if one had killed all of humanity.
scramble.arabic: مِنْ أَجْلِ ذَٰلِكَ كَتَبْنَا عَلَىٰ بَنِىٓ إِسْرَٰٓءِيلَ أَنَّهُۥ مَن قَتَلَ نَفْسًۢا بِغَيْرِ نَفْسٍ أَوْ فَسَادٍۢ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ فَكَأَنَّمَا قَتَلَ ٱلنَّاسَ جَمِيعًۭا وَمَنْ أَحْيَاهَا فَكَأَنَّمَآ أَحْيَا ٱلنَّاسَ جَمِيعًۭا ۚ وَلَقَدْ جَآءَتْهُمْ رُسُلُنَا بِٱلْبَيِّنَـٰتِ ثُمَّ إِنَّ كَثِيرًۭا مِّنْهُم بَعْدَ ذَٰلِكَ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ لَمُسْرِفُونَ
words: مِنْ أَجْلِ ذَٰلِكَ كَتَبْنَا عَلَىٰ بَنِىٓ إِسْرَٰٓءِيلَ أَنَّهُۥ
مَن قَتَلَ نَفْسًۢا بِغَيْرِ نَفْسٍ أَوْ فَسَادٍۢ فِى
ٱلْأَرْضِ فَكَأَنَّمَا قَتَلَ ٱلنَّاسَ جَمِيعًۭا وَمَنْ أَحْيَاهَا فَكَأَنَّمَآ
أَحْيَا ٱلنَّاسَ جَمِيعًۭا ۚ وَلَقَدْ جَآءَتْهُمْ رُسُلُنَا بِٱلْبَيِّنَـٰتِ ثُمَّ
إِنَّ كَثِيرًۭا مِّنْهُم بَعْدَ ذَٰلِكَ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ لَمُسْرِفُونَ
scramble.verseEn: Because of that, We decreed upon the Children of Israel that whoever kills a soul unless for a soul or for corruption [done] in the land - it is as if he had slain mankind entirely. And whoever saves one - it is as if he had saved mankind entirely. And Our messengers had certainly come to them with clear proofs. Then indeed many of them, [even] after that, throughout the land, were transgressors.
